description: Designed graphic. The background is a close-up photograph of a white computer keyboard. One key in the lower-right area is colored bright red and bears a white broken-heart icon. From the left, a folded U.S. dollar bill (Federal Reserve note, with '$B2' and the serial 'CB 09 37250 A' visible) connects to a metallic USB flash drive connector pointed toward the keyboard. Bold black headline at top-left reads: 'Don't lose it all for love.' White bold body text at lower-right on the dark keyboard reads: 'Romance and dating scammers strategically target recently widowed or divorced seniors due to their vulnerabilities and access to cash.' The U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) seal and wordmark appear at bottom-left. Bold sans-serif typography with a red accent.
notable_text: Don't lose it all for love. | Romance and dating scammers strategically target recently widowed or divorced seniors due to their vulnerabilities and access to cash.
